questions.question: A rope wave is traveling horizontally from left to right. In which direction do the individual particles of the rope move?
options: Up and down only
Left and right only
In a circle
Along with the wave to the right
questions.explanation:
Rope waves are transverse; while the wave (energy) travels sideways, the rope particles themselves only move up and down. 

**Common mistake:** Believing the particles move in the same direction as the wave (longitudinal behavior).

questions.question: What does a frequency of $2\text{ Hz}$ mean for a vibrating source?
options: It makes two complete oscillations per second
It takes two seconds to complete one oscillation
The wave travels two metres every second
The wave has a height of two centimetres
questions.explanation:
Frequency is the number of complete waves or oscillations produced per unit time; $2\text{ Hz}$ means two cycles every second. 

**Common mistake:** Confusing frequency with period, which is the time for one oscillation (0.5 s in this case).

questions.question: Compare the motion of particles in a longitudinal wave and a transverse wave. Which statement is correct?
options: Both involve oscillations, but the directions relative to the wave travel are different.
Longitudinal particles move in circles; transverse particles move in straight lines.
Transverse waves require a medium, but longitudinal waves do not.
Only transverse waves can transfer energy without matter.
questions.explanation:
Both wave types involve periodic oscillations that transfer energy; the distinction is whether the oscillation is parallel (longitudinal) or perpendicular (transverse) to the wave direction. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking one type is 'better' at transferring energy than the other.

questions.question: A slinky is stretched out on a floor. To produce a longitudinal pulse, in which direction should the student move the end of the slinky?
options: Forward and backward along the line of the slinky
Side to side at a right angle to the slinky
Up and down away from the floor
In a clockwise circle
questions.explanation:
To create a longitudinal wave, the vibration must be parallel to the direction of propagation, which is achieved by moving the end forward and backward. 

**Common mistake:** Moving it side-to-side, which would produce a transverse wave.

questions.question: How is energy transferred from a moving hand (source) to a rope particle further away?
options: Kinetic energy is passed between adjacent rope particles as the wave progresses.
The hand throws rope molecules toward the far end.
The energy is converted into magnetic potential energy in the rope.
Air molecules carry the energy alongside the rope.
questions.explanation:
According to the notes, the kinetic energy from the source is transferred to rope particles near it, which then pass it to particles further away as the wave progresses. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking the energy travels independently of the particles.
